stem: Your workload is a set of independent circuits with no feedback between them. A colleague suggests always using a session 'to be safe'. According to IBM's guidance, why is batch usually the better default here?

options.text: Sessions are generally more expensive than batches, so you should use batch whenever you do not need a session's dedicated exclusive access.
Sessions cannot run more than one job, so they cannot handle a set of circuits.
Batch mode guarantees your jobs run before any other user's jobs.
Sessions do not support the SamplerV2 and EstimatorV2 primitives.

explanation.correct: Sessions provide dedicated, exclusive access but at higher usage cost. The docs recommend using batch whenever you don't need that dedicated access — as with a set of independent jobs — because batch delivers the same parallel classical processing and single queue wait more cheaply.

distractors.B: Sessions are explicitly designed for multi-job workloads; a session can run many jobs.
distractors.C: Batch jobs do not get priority over other users; they are not even exclusive. It is sessions that reserve the QPU.
distractors.D: Both primitives run in sessions (and in batches and job mode); the mode is passed via `mode=`.
